_meta.note: Monograph receipts for the grounding fence. Quotes are verbatim from the FDA OTC monograph text (deterministic pdftotext extraction, refs/M012.txt / M013.txt), section-cited, with the public FDA URL. Shown when a source is clicked so the FDA basis is verifiable, not a claimed web page.

acetaminophen.monographTitle: Internal Analgesic, Antipyretic, and Antirheumatic Drug Products for OTC Human Use
acetaminophen.quote: Adults: Oral dosage is 325 to 650 milligrams every 4 hours or 325 to 500 milligrams every 3 hours or 650 to 1,000 milligrams every 6 hours, while symptoms persist, not to exceed 4,000 milligrams in 24 hours, or as directed by a doctor.

aspirin.monographTitle: Internal Analgesic, Antipyretic, and Antirheumatic Drug Products for OTC Human Use
aspirin.quote: Adults: Oral dosage is 325 to 650 milligrams every 4 hours or 325 to 500 milligrams every 3 hours or 650 to 1,000 milligrams every 6 hours, while symptoms persist, not to exceed 4,000 milligrams in 24 hours, or as directed by a doctor.

pseudoephedrine.monographTitle: Cough, Cold, Allergy, Bronchodilator, and Antiasthmatic Drug Products for OTC Human Use
pseudoephedrine.quote: Adults and children 12 years of age and over: 60 milligrams every 4 to 6 hours not to exceed 240 milligrams in 24 hours.

phenylephrine.monographTitle: Cough, Cold, Allergy, Bronchodilator, and Antiasthmatic Drug Products for OTC Human Use
phenylephrine.quote: Adults and children 12 years of age and over: 10 milligrams every 4 hours not to exceed 60 milligrams in 24 hours.

dextromethorphan.monographTitle: Cough, Cold, Allergy, Bronchodilator, and Antiasthmatic Drug Products for OTC Human Use
dextromethorphan.quote: Adults and children 12 years of age and over: Oral dosage is 10 to 20 milligrams every 4 hours or 30 milligrams every 6 to 8 hours, not to exceed 120 milligrams in 24 hours, or as directed by a doctor.

guaifenesin.monographTitle: Cough, Cold, Allergy, Bronchodilator, and Antiasthmatic Drug Products for OTC Human Use
guaifenesin.quote: Adults and children 12 years of age and over: oral dosage is 200 to 400 milligrams every 4 hours not to exceed 2,400 milligrams in 24 hours.

chlorpheniramine.monographTitle: Cough, Cold, Allergy, Bronchodilator, and Antiasthmatic Drug Products for OTC Human Use
chlorpheniramine.quote: Adults and children 12 years of age and over: oral dosage is 4 milligrams every 4 to 6 hours, not to exceed 24 milligrams in 24 hours, or as directed by a doctor.

diphenhydramine.monographTitle: Cough, Cold, Allergy, Bronchodilator, and Antiasthmatic Drug Products for OTC Human Use
diphenhydramine.quote: Adults and children 12 years of age and over: oral dosage is 25 to 50 milligrams every 4 to 6 hours, not to exceed 300 milligrams in 24 hours, or as directed by a doctor.

doxylamine.monographTitle: Cough, Cold, Allergy, Bronchodilator, and Antiasthmatic Drug Products for OTC Human Use
doxylamine.quote: Adults and children 12 years of age and over: oral dosage is 7.5 to 12.5 milligrams every 4 to 6 hours, not to exceed 75 milligrams in 24 hours, or as directed by a doctor.
